name: Chantilly Cream Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8-inch square pan and set aside.
2. In a 4-quart heavy saucepan, stir together sugar, milk, heavy cream, light corn syrup, and salt until well combined.
3. Over moderately low heat, stir constantly to dissolve the sugar completely. Bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Cover and boil for 2 to 3 minutes. Uncover and continue cooking without stirring until the mixture reaches 234°F (soft-ball stage) on a candy thermometer.
5. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in vanilla extract.
6. Beat the mixture with an electric mixer until it starts to thicken.
7. Fold in chopped nuts.
8. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
9. Let set until firm, then cut into 25 squares.
10. Store airtight. Makes approximately 2 pounds.
Per square: 137 calories, 1 gram protein, 19 grams carbohydrates, 7 grams fat, 14 milligrams cholesterol. From Woman's Day 1/15/80 Famous Fannie Farmer Candies and Cookies
